# Automated Assembly Line A1 Safety Control Manual

**Document Number**: SC-2024-001
**Version**: v1.5
**Preparation Date**: February 2024
**Scope of Application**: All operators of Automated Assembly Line A1

---

## 1. General Provisions

### 1.1 Purpose

This manual is formulated to strengthen safety production management of Automated Assembly Line A1, prevent and reduce production safety accidents, and protect employee life safety and physical health.

### 1.2 Scope of Application

This manual applies to all operational activities of Automated Assembly Line A1, including equipment operation, maintenance, inspection and testing.

---

## 2. Hazard Identification

### 2.1 Equipment Hazards

| No. | Hazard Name | Hazard Factors | Possible Consequences |
|-----|-------------|----------------|----------------------|
| 1 | CNC Machining Center | High temperature parts, rotating parts | Burns, mechanical injury |
| 2 | Industrial Robotic Arm | Moving parts, clamping force | Crush injury, impact injury |
| 3 | Cooling System | High pressure liquid, temperature change | Burns, frostbite |
| 4 | Hydraulic Station | High pressure oil, aging seals | High pressure jet injury |
| 5 | Electrical Control Cabinet | Electrical components, high voltage power | Electric shock, electrocution |

### 2.2 Environmental Hazards

| No. | Hazard Name | Hazard Factors | Possible Consequences |
|-----|-------------|----------------|----------------------|
| 1 | Workshop Passageway | Floor oil contamination, object stacking | Falls, trips |
| 2 | Material Stacking Area | Material tipping, instability | Crushing, collision |
| 3 | Noise Area | Equipment operating noise | Hearing damage |
| 4 | Chemical Storage | Toxic and hazardous chemicals | Poisoning, chemical burns |

### 2.3 Behavioral Hazards

| No. | Hazard Name | Hazard Factors | Possible Consequences |
|-----|-------------|----------------|----------------------|
| 1 | Violation of Operations | Violating operating procedures | Equipment damage, personal injury |
| 2 | Fatigue Operations | Continuous work, insufficient rest | Slow reaction, misoperation |
| 3 | Missing Protection | Not wearing protective equipment | Personal injury |

---

## 3. Risk Points and Control Measures

### 3.1 Mechanical Equipment Risks

| Risk Point | Risk Level | Control Measures | Responsible Person |
|------------|-------------|------------------|-------------------|
| CNC Machining Center Operation | Major Risk | 1. Strictly follow operating procedures 2. Wear protective goggles and gloves 3. Prohibit opening protective doors during operation 4. Regularly check protective device effectiveness | Equipment Person in Charge |
| Robotic Arm Operating Area | Major Risk | 1. Set safety fences and warning signs 2. Install light curtain protection devices 3. Confirm safe area is clear before operation 4. Strictly prohibit unauthorized entry into operating area | Team Leader |
| Hydraulic System Maintenance | Greater Risk | 1. Release pressure before maintenance 2. Wear protective gloves and goggles 3. Use professional tools for maintenance 4. Conduct pressure test after maintenance | Maintenance Technician |

### 3.2 Electrical Safety Risks

| Risk Point | Risk Level | Control Measures | Responsible Person |
|------------|-------------|------------------|-------------------|
| Electrical Cabinet Operation | Major Risk | 1. Only qualified electricians with electrical work certificates can operate 2. Perform voltage verification before operation 3. Wear insulating shoes and gloves 4. Prohibit live work | Electrician |
| Temporary Electricity Use | Greater Risk | 1. Complete temporary electricity use approval 2. Use qualified electrical equipment 3. Regularly check line insulation performance 4. Implement one machine one switch one protection | Equipment Person in Charge |
| Static Electricity Protection | General Risk | 1. Wear anti-static work clothes 2. Ensure equipment grounding is reliable 3. Maintain appropriate ambient humidity | Safety Officer |

### 3.3 Chemical Safety Risks

| Risk Point | Risk Level | Control Measures | Responsible Person |
|------------|-------------|------------------|-------------------|
| Coolant Use | Greater Risk | 1. Wear protective gloves and goggles 2. Keep workplace ventilated 3. Avoid direct skin contact 4. Regularly test coolant concentration | Equipment Person in Charge |
| Lubricating Grease | General Risk | 1. Wear protective gloves 2. Avoid contact with mouth and eyes 3. Use special tools for application 4. Collect waste oil for unified disposal | Maintenance Technician |
| Cleaning Agent Use | Greater Risk | 1. Use in ventilated areas 2. Wear gas masks 3. Store away from fire sources 4. Operate according to MSDS requirements | Team Leader |

### 3.4 Environmental Safety Risks

| Risk Point | Risk Level | Control Measures | Responsible Person |
|------------|-------------|------------------|-------------------|
| Passageway Blockage | General Risk | 1. Keep passageways clear 2. Do not stack materials in passageways 3. Clean oil stains and debris promptly 4. Install anti-slip facilities | Team Leader |
| Noise Hazard | General Risk | 1. Wear earplugs or earmuffs 2. Control at noise source 3. Conduct noise testing regularly 4. Arrange work time reasonably | Safety Officer |
| Material Stacking | Greater Risk | 1. Stack neatly and stably 2. Do not exceed specified height 3. Leave safety passages 4. Regularly check stacking status | Warehouse Keeper |

---

## 4. Personal Protective Equipment

### 4.1 Required Protective Equipment

| Protective Equipment | Applicable Area | Replacement Cycle | Responsible Person |
|---------------------|-----------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Safety Helmet | All work areas | Replace when damaged | Individual Employee |
| Protective Goggles | Processing area, maintenance area | Replace when damaged | Individual Employee |
| Protective Gloves | When contacting chemicals, mechanical equipment | Replace when damaged | Individual Employee |
| Earplugs/Earmuffs | Noise areas | Replace weekly | Individual Employee |
| Insulating Shoes | Electrical work areas | Replace every 6 months | Individual Employee |
| Anti-static Work Clothes | All work areas | Replace every 1 year | Individual Employee |

### 4.2 Protective Equipment Usage Regulations

1. Must correctly wear protective equipment before work
2. Must immediately replace damaged or ineffective protective equipment
3. Strictly prohibit unauthorized modification or removal of protective devices
4. Must return protective equipment when leaving position or transferring

---

## 5. Safety Responsibility System

### 5.1 Safety Responsibilities at All Levels

| Role | Safety Responsibilities |
|------|----------------------|
| General Manager | Fully responsible for company safety production work |
| Safety Supervisor | Responsible for safety management system construction, safety supervision and inspection |
| Equipment Person in Charge | Responsible for equipment safe operation, hazard investigation and rectification |
| Team Leader | Responsible for team safety management, employee safety education |
| Operators | Strictly follow safety procedures, correctly use protective equipment |

### 5.2 Safety Assessment and Rewards/Penalties

- Conduct monthly safety performance assessment
- Reward promptly reporting discovered hazards
- Penalize violations according to regulations
- Commend annual safety advanced individuals

---

## 6. Hazard Investigation and Rectification

### 6.1 Hazard Investigation Frequency

| Investigation Type | Frequency | Responsible Person |
|-------------------|-----------|-------------------|
| Daily Inspection | Daily | On-duty Employee |
| Weekly Inspection | Weekly | Team Leader |
| Monthly Inspection | Monthly | Equipment Person in Charge |
| Special Inspection | Quarterly | Safety Supervisor |

### 6.2 Hazard Rectification Process

1. Discover hazard, record hazard information
2. Assess hazard level, determine rectification measures
3. Issue rectification notice, implement rectification responsibility
4. Implement rectification, accept rectification results
5. Close file, continuous improvement

---

**Prepared by**: Safety and Environmental Protection Department
**Reviewed by**: Production Department
**Approved by**: General Manager
**Effective Date**: February 15, 2024
